title: Shri Kashi Vishwanath Temple, Varanasi
intro: The Shri Kashi Vishwanath Temple in Varanasi, Uttar Pradesh, is one of the most sacred and famous temples in India dedicated to Lord Shiva.
description:
Shri Kashi Vishwanath Temple, Varanasi
Introduction
The Shri Kashi Vishwanath Temple in Varanasi, Uttar Pradesh, is one of the most sacred and famous temples in India dedicated to Lord Shiva. This temple is one of the 12 Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva and is considered an extremely significant pilgrimage site in Hinduism.
Varanasi (Kashi) is known as the city of Lord Shiva. It is believed that visiting and worshipping Lord Vishwanath in Kashi paves the way for spiritual peace and liberation (Moksha).
Temple Information
 Temple Name: Shri Kashi Vishwanath Temple
 Other Names: Kashi Vishwanath Dham, Vishwanath Temple
 Location: Lahori Tola, Varanasi, Uttar Pradesh 221001, India
 Presiding Deity: Lord Shiva (in Jyotirlinga form)
 Significance: One of the 12 major Jyotirlingas
 Nearest Major City: Varanasi
History and Significance
The Kashi Vishwanath Temple is a major center of the ancient tradition of Lord Shiva worship. Varanasi is considered one of the oldest living cities in the world, and its religious significance dates back thousands of years.
Lord Shiva resides here as Vishwanath (Lord of the Universe). Devotees have deep faith that by the grace of Lord Shiva in Kashi, one attains spiritual progress and the path to liberation.
How to Reach
✈️ By Air
Lal Bahadur Shastri International Airport (VNS)
 Distance from the temple: Approximately 26 km.
 Taxis and cabs are available from the airport to the temple area.
🚆 By Train
Varanasi Junction (Cantt Station)
 Distance from the temple: Approximately 5 km.
Banaras Railway Station
 Distance from the temple: Approximately 6 km.
Pt. Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Junction
 Distance from the temple: Approximately 18 km.
🛣️ By Road
Varanasi is well-connected by road to major cities like Lucknow, Prayagraj, Patna, and others. Taxis, auto-rickshaws, and e-rickshaws are easily available to reach the temple.
Best Time to Visit
For Varanasi Visit:
October to March is considered the best time as the weather is pleasant.
Special Religious Occasions:
 Maha Shivratri
 Shravan Maas (Sawan)
 Dev Deepawali
 Kartik Purnima
Temple Timings
Darshan Timings:
🕒 3:00 AM to 11:00 PM
(Note: Timings may change during festivals and special occasions. Please check the current timings before your visit.)
Special Aartis
Major Aartis performed at the Kashi Vishwanath Temple:
 Mangala Aarti
 Bhog Aarti
 Sandhya Aarti
 Shringar Bhog Aarti
 Shayan Aarti
Nearby Attractions
 Dashashwamedh Ghat: The main ghat famous for Ganga Aarti.
 Ganga Aarti: A world-famous spiritual tradition of Varanasi.
 Annapurna Temple: Famous temple dedicated to Goddess Annapurna.
 Kaal Bhairav Temple: Famous temple of Lord Kaal Bhairav, the protector of Kashi.
 Manikarnika Ghat: One of the oldest and holiest ghats in Varanasi.
Dress Code
There is no specific mandatory dress code for the temple, but devotees are advised to wear modest and traditional clothing.
Facilities for Devotees
 Darshan arrangements
 Security arrangements
 Help centers
 Nearby hotels and Dharmashalas (guesthouses)
 Local transport facilities

faq.q1: Which deity is the Kashi Vishwanath Temple dedicated to?
faq.a1: This temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva and is one of the 12 Jyotirlingas.
faq.q2: Where is the Kashi Vishwanath Temple located?
faq.a2: It is located in Varanasi, Uttar Pradesh.
faq.q3: Which is the nearest airport to Kashi Vishwanath Temple?
faq.a3: Lal Bahadur Shastri International Airport, Varanasi.
faq.q4: What are the darshan timings for Kashi Vishwanath Temple?
faq.a4: Generally, the temple is open for darshan from 3:00 AM to 11:00 PM.
faq.q5: Which places can one visit near the Kashi Vishwanath Temple?
faq.a5:
Dashashwamedh Ghat, Ganga Aarti, Annapurna Temple, Kaal Bhairav Temple, and Manikarnika Ghat are the major attractions.
Conclusion
The Shri Kashi Vishwanath Temple is one of the holiest abodes of Lord Shiva. This temple is not just a religious site but a symbol of Indian spiritual tradition, culture, and faith. For every devotee visiting Varanasi, the darshan of Kashi Vishwanath is considered a special spiritual experience of a lifetime.
